text: What if your software could do things — not just answer, but act?
AI agents are autonomous programs that sense their world, make decisions, and take action.
They follow a simple loop: perceive the situation, think through options, then act. Then they repeat.
Key traits: autonomy — they work without constant supervision. Goal-driven — they pursue specific objectives. Adaptive — they learn from experience.
Customer support bots that resolve tickets. Coding assistants that write and test software. Smart home controllers that manage your environment.
Large language models supercharged agents. Now they understand context, reason through problems, and use tools.
AI agents are your digital workforce. They don't just process information — they act on it.
